title: Copyright year inconsistency: main 2009-2024, ScannerSane files end at 2013
author: sporteka2
body:
The Debian copyright file for gImageReader shows:
- Main (Files: *): Copyright 2009-2024
- Files: gtk/src/scanner/ScannerSane.cc gtk/src/scanner/ScannerSane.hh: Copyright 2009-2013

The scanner source files have copyright ending at 2013 while the project main copyright goes to 2024. Please review and update if needed.

author: birgersp
body: Installed with flatpak. (https://flathub.org/en/apps/io.github.manisandro.gImageReader) Installed on a Ubuntu 24 (GNOME) computer. On launching the application, I get this popup:

title: Please add keyboard shortcuts, Copy Pasting with control V and a Snippet Tool
author: StarWarsExpert
body:
Hello there. Could the devs please add keyboard shortcuts? As far as I know, there are none in the g Image Reader yet.
It would increase the efficency of my image workflow much more if I could use shortcuts.
I often want to extract only a part of a page into text, so that the AI can read it better and I only get the things I want written out. The normal screenshot way doesn't allow that.
So instead I have to use lightshot, select the area, then go to g Image reader, click on the button and paste it in. Then I have to click on read and paste out the text. That's 5 steps.
If I could just use hotkeys, it would be faster. Like Copy Pasting with control V.

A snipping tool would also allow me to select the area in the reader and have it automatically pasted in. That would save time too.
What do you think of these suggestions?

title: Clang incompatibility
author: Carmina16
body: Please replace all the forward declarations of "`std::thread`" with the inclusion of `<thread>`. The former are incompatible with clang compiler.

title: Screenshot doesn't work on GNOME/Wayland
author: vitlav
body:
The "Take screenshot" function fails on GNOME with Wayland session.

  Environment
  - ALT Linux (Sisyphus/p11)
  - GNOME with Wayland
  - gimagereader-gtk 3.4.3

  Steps to reproduce
  1. Open gimagereader-gtk on GNOME (Wayland session)
  2. Files → Take screenshot

  Result: Error "Failed to take screenshot"

  Expected: Screenshot is captured

  Note: Works correctly on XFCE (X11)

  Root cause

  The code in gtk/src/SourceManager.cc uses X11-specific approach:

```
  Glib::RefPtr<Gdk::Window> root = Gdk::Window::get_default_root_window();
  int x, y, w, h;
  root->get_origin(x, y);
  w = root->get_width();
  h = root->get_height();

```
  On Wayland, get_default_root_window() returns a window with zero dimensions because the root window concept doesn't exist in Wayland.

  Suggested fix

  Use xdg-desktop-portal Screenshot interface when running on Wayland.

title: Feature Request: Support DeepSeek OCR and other open-weights HuggingFace OCR Models
author: TheAlex25
body:
Greetings developers. I rarely use OCR, but have noticed that for now, most OCR tools for open-weights AI are CLI, besides gImageReader, which uses GUI (as I prefer GUIs). It would be nice if gImageReader could support more than just Tesseract, and allow users to download HuggingFace OCR models. Overall, I am looking forward to the future of open-source self-hosted AI.

Here is a list of models that could be listed in an OCR model picker.
OlmOCR: https://huggingface.co/allenai/olmOCR-2-7B-1025
PaddleOCR: https://huggingface.co/unsloth/PaddleOCR-VL
DeepSeek OCR: https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR
DotsOCR: https://huggingface.co/rednote-hilab/dots.ocr

I hope you guys consider this feature request, and thank you for making a great self-hosted OCR tool.

Merry Christmas, and happy holidays

Alex B.

title: Order of files
author: femgreen
body:
When I open a folder of files in gImageReader (3.4.2), the files are shown almost in filename order. However the file type seems to be included in the sort.

For example the files:
p005.png, p006.png, p006b.png, p006p.png, p007.png
appear in the order:
p005.png, p006b.png, p006.png, p006p.png, p007.png

but:
p155.png, p156.png, p156p.png, p156r.png, p157.png
appear in the correct order.

If the files are tifs rather than pngs, the same thing occurs, but the sort order goes wrong for suffixes before t rather than p.

I process the files as Multiple pages, prepending the source filename to output text. Having the pages output in the wrong order causes me problems.

I use gImageReader to prepare books for [Distributed Proofreaders](https://www.pgdp.net/), and find it a very useful tool apart from this one glitch.

title: Can't Make Spellcheck Work Ubuntu
author: m00tpoint
body: Apologies but I just can't make this work. I'm Linux Mint. Installed gImageReader from Ubuntu repos, version 3.4.2. It works with tesseract 5 just fine. It can see my model, fraknew, and use it. I see German as a dropdown option, and see my several German hunspell dictionaries in its submenu. I have the paths set to user, and ln -s from my ~/.config directories to the system directories, and as noted the app is able to find the models and the spelling dictionaries to populate its menus. I have mapped fraknew to various versions of German under the name OldGerman. But when I recognize a page, and want to go through misspelled words in the hocr output, every word is considered misspelled. The hocr .html output file and the live output in the tool correctly show whatever language code I have mapped in my OldGerman model -> language mapping. But spellcheck just does not work, no matter which German dictionary I choose. Please help!

title: Ubuntu noble updates rejected: "gimagereader does not have a Release file"
author: yarko
body:
In Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS (noble), the ppa repository works for initial install, but then is rejected by `apt update`:

```
Reading package lists... Done
E: The repository 'https://ppa.launchpadcontent.net/sandromani/gimagereader/ubuntu noble Release' does not have a Release file.
N: Updating from such a repository can't be done securely, and is therefore disabled by default.
N: See apt-secure(8) manpage for repository creation and user configuration details.
```

title: Help Menu for Ubuntu
author: Ivan2getdmodelz
body: The Help dropdown from the settings gear doesn't launch any help menu or provide any new information.  It would be very useful to have some setting and functional descriptions of what the different processing and opitions (multi-lingual, Page segmentation, for plain text processing whole page vs. autodetect for Recognition Area, etc.)

title: Add graphic region for OCR - feature location unclear
author: JMak22
body:
In the Help documentation, it is stated that "New graphic areas can be defined by selecting the _Add graphic region_ entry of the context menu of the respective page item and drawing a rectangle on the canvas." The exact location of the feature "Add graphic region" is unclear, based on the visual information available in the GUI, from inspection of various elements and their mouse hover text. 

Please specify exact location of feature as well as under which conditions it becomes available (ex. when document is loaded, after OCR has been applied to a document, if any specific panes should be visible etc.)

Including visual information (screenshots, gifs, walk-throughs) in the Help documentation, outlining basic functions would be extremely helpful. I would be happy to provide the images/animations, I would need some guidance as to which would be helpful, other than the ones I have used so far.

Thank you!

author: MenonRB
body:
Have installed the Flatpak version, and it works well. Thank you.

When doing a command-line update, the following error comes up:

Info: runtime org.gnome.Platform branch 46 is end-of-life, with reason:
   The GNOME 46 runtime is no longer supported as of April 17, 2025. Please ask your application developer to migrate to a supported platform.
Info: applications using this runtime:
   io.github.manisandro.gImageReader

Does this need to be updated within the flatpak version itself or can a user do something to change the GNOME version.

Thanks

title: Add default image processing settings manageable from a settings file
author: StanislavKoncebovski
body:
My experience with gImageReader is that it works much better if some settings (such as contrast and resolution) are selected at a higher value. Typically, for the kind of documents I scan (typewritten pages, books, etc.) the contrast has to be set to the value of ~50 and the resolution to 300 dpi. If the contrast is too low (default = 0), Tesseract tends to find characters where there are none.
Since there is no option to save the default values, I need to do the same for each session anew.
It would be great to have the possibility to save the default values of brightness, contrast, resolution and maybe the rotation angle in a config file, with a dialog to change the values from within the application. Not much work, as far as I understand.

title: hocr editor: page image should be in the background
author: milahu
body:
clicking on the page in the center widget
does always focus the page image in the hocr tree

expected:
clicking a text node in the center widget
should focus the text node in the hocr tree

doubleclicking a text node in the center widget
should start editing the word text in the hocr tree

screenshot:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5efd41d5-d8eb-4066-b3e0-cc14cc635070)

when i remove the "graphic" node (page image)
then i get the same issue with the page node:
no matter where i click in the center view, i can only focus the page node

possible solution:
probably the page image should be rendered as a background image
and "graphic" nodes should only be used for subsections of the page
with non-text (graphical) content

example hocr and image files are in #731

gimagereader version c05e8c8c22e73b5f502f1f1df669aed1433c9a36

part of #438
